what_it_does: This file is the command-line entry point for the recollection sync tool. It parses process.argv, guards against reentrant summarizer-spawned runs, prints help, optionally forks a detached background process, and then either runs a regular incremental sync across configured conversation source directories or executes a bulk backfill subcommand. It acquires a single-instance file lock, aggregates sync results, prints a summary, and runs a post-sync embedding migration batch for stale rows.
observations: Reentrancy guard at the very top exits silently if the sync was triggered inside a summarizer-spawned subprocess.
Help text is extensive and distinguishes normal sync from the backfill subcommand.
Background mode filters out --background, opens a log file, spawns a detached child with stdio redirected to that log, and exits immediately.
Regular sync checks source directories, acquires a file lock, syncs each source, prints totals, and then runs an embedding migration phase.
Backfill uses its own separate lock file so it does not block normal sync, but two backfills cannot run concurrently.
Backfill options --limit/--max-convs, --batch-size, and --project are parsed with small helper functions.
The backfill module is imported dynamically only when the subcommand runs.
Signal and exit handlers release the acquired file lock exactly once using a done flag.
Migration phase uses an environment variable EPISODIC_MEMORY_MIGRATION_BATCH with a default of 500.
standouts: Reentrancy guard to prevent cascading Claude subprocess fanout (#87).
Background mode forks a detached child for hook-fired invocations.
Separate single-instance locks for regular sync and backfill.
Post-sync embedding migration phase integrated into normal sync.
conventions: Node CLI using process.argv slice and manual argument parsing.
File locking for single-instance concurrency control.
console.error for diagnostics to keep stdout clean for MCP/consumer use.
Dynamic import for optional heavy subcommand modules.
Signal handlers for graceful resource release.
concerns: releaseSyncLockOnce and releaseOnce attach a done property via `as any` instead of a typed closure variable or WeakSet.
syncAll and runEmbeddingMigrationPhase are declared inside the else block; while function declarations are hoisted within the block, this block-scoped placement is slightly unusual.
No validation that --background combined with --help behaves as expected, though it likely exits before forking.
The parent process opens a log file descriptor and never explicitly closes it, relying on process exit.
notes: This is the hook-safe CLI orchestrator: it prevents sync from spawning itself recursively, supports background detachment, enforces single-instance execution, and finishes regular sync with an embedding migration pass.
questions: What exact environment or state does shouldSkipReentrantSync inspect?
How does acquireFileLock behave across Windows and POSIX, and what is the lock holder format?
How does backfillConversations track and resume from a per-archive high-water-mark?
What criteria make an exchange row 'stale' for migration?
Why is the embedding migration phase only run after regular sync and not after backfill?
What is the full relationship between syncConversations and the summarizer subprocess it may spawn?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it syncs conversations, supports background hooks, provides a bulk backfill, and performs embedding maintenance. The added reentrancy guard and locking are defensive extensions of that purpose.
apparent_themes: Incremental sync plus bulk corpus ingestion
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the sync command safe to invoke automatically from editor hooks: avoid recursive subprocess explosions, avoid concurrent SQLite writes, run without blocking the parent, and keep the embedding store consistent over model changes.
novelty: The combination of a reentrancy guard against summarizer-spawned subprocesses, a dedicated backfill lock path, and an integrated post-sync embedding migration phase is distinctive.
contribution: This file contributes the top-level CLI routing and operational safeguards for the recollection sync system: argument parsing, help, background forking, reentrancy suppression, single-instance locking, result aggregation, and the embedding migration phase.
summary_for_embedding: CLI entry point for the recollection sync tool that parses subcommands, guards against reentrant summarizer-triggered runs, supports background detached execution, runs regular incremental sync with file locking, and executes a bulk backfill subcommand.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a robust, hook-safe command-line interface for syncing and backfilling conversation archives while preventing cascading subprocess fanout and concurrent SQLite races.

symbols.does: Releases the regular sync file lock exactly once on process exit or signals.
symbols.description: A closure that calls releaseFileLock for the regular sync lock and uses a done flag to prevent double-release when multiple exit/signal events fire.

symbols.does: Iterates over all configured conversation source directories and aggregates sync results.
symbols.description: An async function that calls syncConversations for each source directory, accumulates copied, skipped, indexed, summarized, and error totals, prints a summary, and then triggers the embedding migration phase.

symbols.does: Re-embeds up to a batch size of stale exchange rows after regular sync completes.
symbols.description: Opens the database, counts rows still on the old embedding model, initializes the embedding stack, runs a bounded migration batch, logs how many were re-embedded, and closes the database.

symbols.does: Parses a positive integer CLI argument by one or more flag names.
symbols.description: Scans args for any of the given flag names and returns the parsed positive integer from the following token, or undefined if the flag is missing or invalid.

symbols.does: Parses a string CLI argument by flag name.
symbols.description: Scans args for the given flag and returns the following token as a string, or undefined if the flag is missing.

symbols.does: Acquires a separate backfill lock and invokes the bulk backfill subcommand.
symbols.description: Parses backfill-specific options, acquires a dedicated single-instance lock, dynamically imports the backfill module, awaits backfillConversations, prints the resulting statistics, and handles errors.

symbols.does: Releases the backfill file lock exactly once on process exit or signals.
symbols.description: A closure that releases the backfill lock and uses a done flag to ensure the lock is released only once across process exit and signal handlers.
